description:
Goal
Derive ABI-ready parameter and return layouts from the settled runtime demand plan instead of from semantic activation inputs.

Problem being fixed
src/compiler2/jobs/artifact.rs currently builds ABI params with key.activation.input.map(abi_value_repr). That treats every semantic input as a runtime ABI lane and forces unused values, including unused callable values, into later representation work.

Code seams
- src/compiler2/jobs/artifact.rs: MaterializedExecutable, AbiReadyExecutable, build_executable_abi_plan, settle_return_abis, propagate_resume_value_reprs, resolve_entry_return_abi.
- src/compiler2/jobs/backend.rs: entry_input_abis and collect_entry_input_need already contain a smaller backward demand engine for delivered resume values; keep the useful idea but move the authority to the shared runtime demand plan.

Data model to derive
RuntimeInputLayout = Omitted { semantic_index } | Value { semantic_index, repr } | TupleFields { semantic_index, fields } | DirectCallableCaptures { semantic_index, captures }.
ABI-ready programs should carry runtime input layouts and value reprs derived from RuntimeDemand, not a one-to-one semantic input list.

Algorithm
1. For each executable, read ExecutableRuntimeDemand.input_demands.
2. Convert Ignore into Omitted lanes.
3. Convert Value into normal AbiValueRepr lanes.
4. Convert TupleFields into field lanes using the same return/resume delivery semantics that make quicksort efficient.
5. Convert direct-only callable demand into capture lanes when the callable producer is known and non-escaping.
6. Leave opaque/escaping callable demand as first-class Value/Callable lanes because a runtime callable object is required.

Invariant
Callable-entry ABI is a boundary contract for first-class callable values. It is not the executable-internal return or resume ABI.

Removal target
Remove the assumption that every semantic ActivationKey.input element becomes an ABI parameter. Collapse or delete backend-local resume demand recomputation once artifact planning owns the layout.

Checks
- Existing quicksort tuple-field tests remain green and still show field delivery instead of ListTail-style whole-value over-allocation.
- A fixture with an unused callable semantic input has no runtime parameter lane for that input.
- A fixture with a direct captured lambda passes only demanded captures, not a heap closure.
- ABI-ready telemetry reports omitted_inputs > 0 for the unused-callable case.
title: Compiler2 ABI planning: derive runtime parameter layouts from demand
